_meta.title: Content family — design INTAKE (ui-code + the hyperlink capability + ui-disclosure; scope ruling + first records; no build)
_meta.author: system-planner
_meta.grounding: Forcing evidence: agents emit code constantly and the site centralizes its own hand-rolled <pre><code> (site/lib/code-block.ts + 6 page consumers — instant dogfood); no href exists anywhere in catalog.json (grep-verified 2026-07-08) so an agent structurally cannot emit a hyperlink; long agent reports need folding and no fold vocabulary exists in fleet or catalog. Upstream: ADR-0107 + chart PRD (the intake shape) · ADR-0078 (the ui-text axis charter; cl.4 stamp mechanics; ADR-0106/0109 the fourth/fifth-axis precedents) · ADR-0102 (the three-lane chooser) · ADR-0087 (SPEC-N2 whole-fleet gate) · ADR-0097 + Amendment (the TOTAL feed partition — dispositions owed) · the catalog security posture (SPEC-R9 allowlist, callableFrom clientOnly default, ADR-0098 static-literal validation lane) · references/geometry.md (five size-classes; 'accordion' already listed under Pattern).
_meta.note: Both planes derived independently: OUTSIDE-IN structure from the intake record family (scope ruling -> per-member contract directions -> ecosystem dispositions -> records -> gates/handback); INSIDE-OUT actions from the dispatch's design questions (code content model / highlighting fence / copy fence, hyperlink vehicle + security stack, disclosure vehicle + state contract, geometry placement) + the standing doctrine set (zero-dep, SPEC-N2 gate, ADR-0097 partition, ADR-0101 announce law, ADR-0102 chooser, handoff contract). Coordinator-reserved numbers 0113/0114 (siblings hold 0111/0112; 0108 is a known hole — numbers are never improvised).

nodes.label: v1 scope ruling (members in + fences out, reasons from mechanics)
nodes.accept: the PRD scope section names the v1-IN members AND the ruled-out set with a per-item reason; harness_checks.py prd P3 (out-of-scope stated) passes as part of exit 0

nodes.label: contract directions (the what-direction the ADRs record; SPEC/LLD own mechanisms at build)
ui-code contract direction (content model + overflow law + inert language + the highlighting/copy fences)
nodes.accept: ADR-0113 Decision states the ui-code content model (host-as-content vs code-prop weighed against the textFactory clobber/heal mechanics), overflow-x scrolling owned BY the component citing ADR-0102, language as inert v1 metadata, and highlighting + copy-to-clipboard FENCED with a named escape hatch each

nodes.label: hyperlink capability direction (ui-text `as` gains 'a' + href — not a new tag) + the navigation-security stack
nodes.accept: ADR-0114 exists at the reserved number, records as='a'+href over a new-tag alternative, a component-enforced scheme allowlist with fail-closed behavior on violation, the rel/target policy, the bindability ruling, and the two-line defense (validator leg = first line, component = last line)

nodes.label: ui-disclosure vehicle + state contract (native <details>/<summary> wrap vs bespoke; open prop; summary model)
nodes.accept: ADR-0113 Decision rules the disclosure vehicle from mechanics (styling constraints, `name` exclusive-accordion, find-in-page auto-expand, whether the no-native-form-elements law binds), records `open` as a reflected prop under the ADR-0101 always-announce law, and rules summary-as-prop vs first-child content

nodes.label: geometry/size-class placement for both controls
nodes.accept: ADR-0113 Decision names the geometry.md size class each control joins (Display for ui-code; Pattern for ui-disclosure — the table's own 'accordion' row) and the --ui-{code,disclosure}-* token direction

nodes.label: catalog rows + Gen-UI teaching disposition (SPEC-N2/ADR-0087 same-wave path + §5.2 guidance + exemplar)
nodes.accept: ADR-0113 Decision states the same-wave catalog-row-or-allowlist path (end-of-wave allowlist residue: none), the three row shapes (Code display-only, Disclosure with the two-way open mark, Text gaining href per ADR-0114), and names the report exemplar + §5.2 usage-guidance prose

nodes.label: feed-partition dispositions (ADR-0097 total partition — mechanically owed)
nodes.accept: ADR-0113 records an IN-or-OUT+reason disposition for Code and Disclosure, and notes that Text.href rides Text's existing IN membership consciously

nodes.label: adjacent consequences (site dogfood follow-up consumer + anticipated size re-base)
nodes.accept: the PRD/ADR-0113 name the site code-block replacement as a booked FOLLOW-UP consumer outside this wave's scope, and the family-size-budget re-base as an anticipated, measured-at-build consequence (the ADR-0107 Amendment precedent)

nodes.label: sibling-vs-extension ruling + the compact PRD (.claude/docs/prd/content-family.prd.md)
nodes.accept: the PRD file exists under .claude/docs/prd/ with stable PRD-G IDs, metric+baseline+target+timeframe per goal, both-sided scope, priority tiers, and the sibling-vs-extension ruling stated in its header; harness_checks.py prd <path> exits 0

nodes.label: scoping ADR-0113 (proposed; Kim forks with firm recommendations)
nodes.accept: 0113-content-family-v1-scope.md exists under .claude/docs/adr/ with Status: proposed (never self-flipped — the hook enforces), non-empty Repairs, all four required sections + Acceptance, and >=3 named Kim forks each carrying a firm recommendation; adr_check.py <path> exits 0

nodes.label: hyperlink ADR-0114 (proposed; the own-record ruling honored)
nodes.accept: 0114-text-hyperlink-href.md exists at the reserved number with Status: proposed, Extends ADR-0078 resolving under adr_check C8, the security clauses present, and >=2 named Kim forks with firm recommendations; adr_check.py <path> exits 0

nodes.label: index rows (adr/README.md — re-read the tail immediately before edit; concurrent siblings own 0111/0112)
nodes.accept: adr/README.md index table carries 0113 + 0114 rows (title + status + repairs) appended without disturbing any sibling row; adr_check C6 (indexed) passes for both

nodes.label: mechanical harness gates run to green
nodes.accept: coverage_check.py exits 0 on this manifest AND harness_checks.py prd exits 0 AND adr_check.py exits 0 on both ADRs, all actually run this session with output read (never eyeballed)

nodes.label: design-status handback (handoff-compose contract)
nodes.accept: the final handback carries the eight handoff fields in order, Status on line 1, gate verdicts by command, and names doc-reviewer as the ratifying critic

actions.label: decide which content members earn v1 and which are fenced out, argued from mechanics not fashion
decide ui-code's content model + overflow ownership + the highlighting and copy-affordance fences
decide the hyperlink vehicle: extend ui-text's `as` axis + href vs mint a new tag
decide the href security stack: scheme allowlist, enforcement point(s), rel/target policy, bindability
decide the disclosure vehicle (native details wrap vs bespoke button+region) + the open/summary contract
place both controls in the five-size-class geometry taxonomy
disposition the catalog rows + the teaching exemplar under the whole-fleet gate
disposition the ADR-0097 feed partition for every new/widened type
book the site dogfood follow-up consumer and anticipate the size re-base
rule PRD-sibling-vs-extension and author the compact PRD with gated goals
record the family scope decision as proposed ADR-0113 naming the genuine Kim forks
rule whether the hyperlink earns its own record and author proposed ADR-0114
index the new records so every citation resolves, without disturbing concurrent siblings
run the mechanical doc gates and fix to green
compose the handback naming the reviewer seat

edges.why: the PRD's scope section pins the v1-in/out ruling — it cannot be authored before the ruling exists
ADR-0113's Decision clause 1 records the member-set ruling
the ADR records the ui-code contract direction; deciding it is prerequisite content
the ADR records the disclosure vehicle + state contract; deciding it is prerequisite content
the ADR records the geometry placement; deciding it is prerequisite content
ADR-0114 records the hyperlink vehicle + security stack; deciding them is prerequisite content
the catalog/exemplar disposition is ADR-0113 Decision content
the feed-partition disposition is ADR-0113 Decision content
the follow-up consumer + size-re-base consequence land in the PRD's scope/milestones
ADR-0113's Repairs field pins the PRD and its clauses cite PRD-G IDs — the PRD must exist first
ADR-0114's clauses trace to PRD-G2 — the PRD must exist first
ADR-0114 cross-references the family scope record that admits the hyperlink into v1
the index row cites ADR-0113's number + title
the index row cites ADR-0114's number + title
the PRD gate runs over the authored PRD
the ADR gate runs over authored ADR-0113
the ADR gate runs over authored ADR-0114
adr_check C6 asserts the README index rows exist — the gate can only pass after indexing
the handback reports gate verdicts by command — the gates must have run first
